chunk_summary: This section outlines malnutrition classification and growth trajectory triggers in children with chronic cardiorespiratory disease, emphasizing z-scores, weight velocity, and context-specific adjustments for accurate assessment.
learning_objectives: Understand malnutrition classification criteria for children with chronic cardiorespiratory disease.
Learn to apply z-scores, weight velocity, and MUAC for severity assessment.
Identify growth trajectory triggers and their implications for nutritional interventions.
Recognize the importance of context-specific adjustments, such as corrected age and edema, in malnutrition assessment.
key_facts: Severe malnutrition in children <2 years: weight-for-length z-score (WFLz) < -3 or bilateral pitting edema.
Severe malnutrition in children 2–20 years: BMI-for-age z-score (BMIz) < -3.
MUAC thresholds for severe malnutrition (6–59 months): <115 mm or edema; moderate: 115–124 mm.
Growth trajectory triggers include WFLz/BMIz drop by ≥1 over 4–12 weeks or weight gain velocity <50% of expected in infancy.
Corrected age must be used for ex-preterm infants up to 24 months to avoid inappropriate nutritional interventions.
reference_quote: Severe acute malnutrition in children aged 6–59 months is defined as MUAC <115 mm or the presence of bilateral pitting edema. (Source: https://iapindia.org/pdf/Ch-136-Severe-Acute-Malnutrition.pdf)
document_key_topics: Malnutrition classification in chronic cardiorespiratory disease
